name: Edward
background: Second year CS student at University of Leicester, currently 67% (2:1). Autism and ADHD diagnosed. Systems thinker — understands things through structure and pattern, not memorisation. Strong lateral thinking and cross-domain pattern recognition. Thinks in frameworks and transfers knowledge across domains naturally.
focus_areas: Cybersecurity and penetration testing — working toward red teaming
Building Jarvis AI assistant using Claude API
CTF practice — OverTheWire (Bandit level 20+), HackTheBox, Pwn College
Muay Thai and BJJ — 3 years training, clinch-oriented Muay Thai, half guard chain in BJJ
Chess — used as a structural thinking domain
learning_style: Explicit over intuitive. Needs structure and reasoning before application. Comes alive on projects and applied problems. Disengages from passive consumption. Understands things by mapping their structure, not memorising details.
communication_preferences: Direct and honest. Concrete next actions over conceptual exploration. Call out intellectualising, over-planning, or avoidance disguised as thinking. No excessive formatting or bullet-point-heavy responses. Match depth — can handle nuance and complexity.
current_priorities: Finish university coursework without last-minute panic
Build Jarvis to Phase 2 and get it on GitHub
CTF sessions — OverTheWire Natas and HackTheBox
patterns_to_watch: All-or-nothing thinking — two weeks hard then nothing
Over-planning as avoidance — building systems instead of doing the thing
Intellectualising under stress — going analytical when something is emotionally hard
Maladaptive daydreaming — hours in his head without output
Task initiation gap — knows what to do but struggles to start it
Shame spirals after wasted days — honest accounting is useful, shame is not
strengths: Strong in interviews and high-pressure conversations
Systems thinking applied across BJJ, chess, cybersecurity, and software
Self-aware about his own patterns and willing to be called out

context.university_project: Spring Boot REST API with security, RBAC, MySQL, HTTPS, JUnit tests
